title: I have to dislose where I live because I do Google Earth anyways & Strava

enrichment.summary: Kyle explains why he is disclosing his home address (a Boulder, CO condo at Remington Coz), arguing that privacy fears are irrational and that Strava and his Google Earth 'Boulder Rome' project would reveal his location anyway. He walks through his Boulder Rome project — a mapping of ancient Roman cities and battles onto Colorado geography — and shows his neighborhood, apartment window, and speculative future-Boulder 3D builds to justify why working from his actual location is logistically necessary.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: necessary and right — hiding it is irrational fear, not real safety
overrated; determined people can find anyone's address anyway
dismisses them as fear-based projection with no logical merit
would accept them as 'street cred' for acting on principle
rejects it as projection; he just wants to be free and avoid hiding things
plans to live there the rest of his life and never travel
